statement.en: Trust-SSL: Additive-Residual Selective Invariance for Robust Aerial Self-Supervised Learning
statement.summary: Self-supervised learning (SSL) is a standard approach for representation learning in aerial imagery. Existing methods enforce invariance between augmented views, which works well when augmentations preserve semantic content. However, aerial images are frequently degraded by haze, motion blur, rain, and occlusion that remove critical evidence. Enforcing alignment between a clean and a severely degraded view can introduce spurious structure into the latent space. This study proposes a training strategy and architectural modification to enhance SSL robustness to such corruptions. It introduces a per-sample, per-factor trust weight into the alignment objective, combined with the base contrastive loss as an additive residual. A stop-gradient is applied to the trust weight instead of a multiplic

proposer: Wadii Boulila, Adel Ammar, Bilel Benjdira et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper proposes novel SSL architecture (Trust-SSL) with trust-weighted alignment for degraded aerial imagery. Claims are empirically testable but: (1) preprint status (2026 date suggests future/speculative), (2) abstract incomplete (cuts mid-sentence), (3) core innovation (trust weights + additive-residual formulation) lacks full methodological exposition in provided text, (4) robustness claims require validation across multiple aerial datasets and corruption types. Not yet established fact, not refuted, but under active development/validation cycle.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 汚れた空の映像でも、信頼の重みが自動的に悪い対応を消す—加算される信頼とは拒否の優雅さである。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 清い球が濁った球とつながるとき、太い線だけが真実を伝え、他は消える—選別される不信が構造を守る。
